name: Jambalaya I
recipeIngredient: 2 bell peppers, stemmed, cored, seeded, and diced
1 medium red onion, quartered into pieces
1 medium white onion, quartered into pieces
1 medium yellow onion, quartered into pieces
1 small bunch fresh basil (or 2 tablespoons dried)
1 small bunch fresh oregano (or 2 tablespoons dried)
recipeInstructions:
1. Puree celery, peppers, onions, garlic, and herbs (except bay leaf) in a food processor.
2. Transfer mixture to a large pot; add canned tomatoes, sun-dried tomatoes, chicken, bay leaves, chile powder, and salt.
3. Cover and bring to a boil over medium heat.
4. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for about 35 minutes until vegetables are tender.
5. Thinly slice the Andouille sausage and roast in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for about 15 minutes to render out grease.
6. Add roasted Andouille sausage, reduce heat to low, and simmer for another 30 minutes.
7. Add shrimp and cook until pink and opaque, about 5 minutes.
8. Season with additional salt and pepper if needed. Serve over steamed rice.
From George Louie. Serves 10-12.
